rulings.Case Title: REBECCA JANECEK VS. MIKE TAM et al

rulings.Case Title: DANGER PANDA, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. LAMAR WALENT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 23, 2014 line 9. DEFENDANT LAMAR WALENT and SCOTT BATY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. Whether Plaintiff unreasonably refused to consent to a sublet of the premises is a question of fact not resolvable via a motion for summary judgment. See Cohen v. Ratinoff (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 321, 330. = (501/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN WILLIAMS VS. MICHAEL BRENDSEL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 23, 2014 line 7. DEFENDANT MICHAEL BRENDSEL's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled 5 days to answer. Notice of Motion is defective. No grounds for the motion are listed. CCP 1010. Notwithstanding tis deficiency, the Court overrules the demurer on the following grounds. Defendant states no authority for the proposition that notice of tenant's rights to re-occupy the premises in excess of what is statutorily required renders the Notice of Termination fatally defective. Defendant's demurrer is not based on the misjoinder of parties under CCP 430.10(d) and no other grounds re: standing to raise the issue are asserted. Omitted claimants have a right to file a claim of right to possession. Good faith and ulterior motive are issues of fact not properly addressed on demurrer. = (501/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUES LANDY VS. ROBERT PIGGOTT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 23, 2014 line 8. DEFENDANT ROBERT PIGGOTT's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges unlawful detainer action. Expiration of Notice is sufficiently alleged. Complaint is not uncertain and the portions of form complaint re: service of notice are sufficiently alleged. Whether habitual late payment is a curable or a non-curable violation is a factual issue. = (501/GLW)
